meta.title: Native-Level Idiomatic Expression & Collocation
meta.description: Master the subtlest idiomatic expressions, collocations, and fixed phrases at C2 level.
meta.description_ko: C2 수준에서 가장 미묘한 관용 표현, 연어, 고정 구문을 학습합니다.

explanation.en: 'At a loose end' (BrE) = having nothing to do; without plans or purpose.
explanation.ko: 'at a loose end'(영국 영어) = 할 일이 없는; 계획이나 목적이 없는.

explanation.en: 'Stillborn' (figurative) = failing at the very start; never realised. Strong metaphor from a birth with no life.
explanation.ko: 'stillborn'(비유) = 바로 처음에 실패한; 결코 실현되지 않은.

questions.prompt: The plan was _____ before it even left the drawing board, killed by bureaucratic inertia.

explanation.en: 'Sail close to the wind' = act in a way that is almost but not quite illegal or acceptable.
explanation.ko: 'sail close to the wind' = 거의 불법적이거나 받아들이기 어려울 정도의 경계에서 행동하다.

questions.prompt: 'to sail close to the wind'의 뜻은?
choices: 위험할 정도로 규칙이나 법의 경계에 가깝게 행동하다

explanation.en: 'Come to nothing' = result in failure. 'Back at square one' = back to the beginning.
explanation.ko: 'come to nothing' = 실패로 끝나다. 'back at square one' = 처음으로 돌아가다.

questions.answer: The merger talks came to nothing, leaving both companies back at square one.

explanation.en: 'Give short shrift to' = deal with dismissively; give little consideration to.
explanation.ko: 'give short shrift to' = 대수롭지 않게 처리하다; 거의 고려하지 않다.

choices: 무언가를 대수롭지 않게 일축하다/거의 고려하지 않다

explanation.en: 'Alienating both sides' = the result of trying to please everyone is pleasing no one.
explanation.ko: 'alienating both sides' = 모든 사람을 기쁘게 하려다 아무도 기쁘게 하지 못하는 결과.

questions.prompt: Her attempt to appease both sides only succeeded in _____ both — a textbook case of trying to please everyone.

explanation.en: 'Get wind of' = hear a rumour or receive information about something, often secret.
explanation.ko: 'get wind of' = 종종 비밀인 무언가에 대한 소문이나 정보를 입수하다.

explanation.en: 'Shattered an image' = completely destroyed a carefully built perception.
explanation.ko: 'shattered an image' = 세심하게 구축된 인식을 완전히 파괴하다.

questions.prompt: The scandal _____ the minister's carefully constructed image of incorruptibility.

explanation.en: 'Rest on one's laurels' = be satisfied with past achievements and stop striving.
explanation.ko: 'rest on one's laurels' = 과거 업적에 만족하고 노력을 멈추다.

choices: 과거의 성공에 안주하여 더 이상 노력하지 않다

explanation.en: 'Cosmetic measure' = superficial change that doesn't address the underlying problem.
explanation.ko: 'cosmetic measure' = 근본적인 문제를 다루지 않는 표면적인 변화.

questions.prompt: The new policy was presented as a _____ measure, but critics argued it would entrench existing inequalities.

explanation.en: 'Moot point' = debatable or of no practical significance (the situation has changed).
explanation.ko: 'moot point' = 논쟁의 여지가 있거나 실질적 중요성이 없는 논점(상황이 변했을 때).

choices: 논쟁의 여지가 있거나 더 이상 관련이 없는 논점

explanation.en: 'Conspicuously sparing with praise' = noticeably restrained; the absence itself is meaningful.
explanation.ko: 'conspicuously sparing with praise' = 눈에 띄게 절제된; 부재 자체가 의미 있다.

questions.prompt: The report was _____ in its praise of the government's handling of the crisis — a fact not lost on observant readers.
choices: conspicuously absent of / conspicuously sparing with

explanation.en: 'Take umbrage at' = be offended or feel resentment at something.
explanation.ko: 'take umbrage at' = 무언가에 기분이 상하거나 불쾌함을 느끼다.

explanation.en: 'Come back to haunt someone' = return as a problem or cause of trouble later.
explanation.ko: 'come back to haunt someone' = 나중에 문제나 골칫거리로 돌아오다.

questions.answer: The committee's decision to suppress the findings will come back to haunt them.

explanation.en: 'Crossing swords' = being in conflict or disagreement; quarrelling.
explanation.ko: 'crossing swords' = 갈등이나 의견 불일치 상태에 있다; 다투다.

questions.prompt: The two factions had been _____ swords for months before the confrontation finally erupted.

explanation.en: 'Pull one's punches' = be less forceful or critical than one could be; hold back.
explanation.ko: 'pull one's punches' = 할 수 있는 것보다 덜 강하거나 비판적으로 행동하다; 자제하다.

explanation.en: 'Hinged on' = depended entirely on; turned on one key factor.
explanation.ko: 'hinged on' = 완전히 의존하다; 하나의 핵심 요소에 달려 있다.

questions.prompt: The prosecution's case _____ on the testimony of a single eyewitness — a precarious foundation.

explanation.en: 'In the offing' = likely to happen soon; in the near future.
explanation.ko: 'in the offing' = 곧 일어날 것 같은; 가까운 미래에.

explanation.en: 'Sidestep a question' = avoid answering directly; evade.
explanation.ko: 'sidestep a question' = 직접 답변을 피하다; 회피하다.

questions.prompt: She _____ the question with characteristic evasiveness, answering a question she hadn't been asked.

explanation.en: 'Keep one's powder dry' = conserve resources for when they are truly needed.
explanation.ko: 'keep one's powder dry' = 진정으로 필요할 때를 위해 자원을 보존하다.

choices: 자원과 에너지를 적절한 때를 위해 보존하다

explanation.en: 'Burned his bridges' = destroyed the possibility of going back; made an irreversible decision.
explanation.ko: 'burned his bridges' = 되돌아갈 가능성을 파괴했다; 되돌릴 수 없는 결정을 내렸다.

questions.answer: Having staked his entire reputation on the outcome, the chairman had burned his bridges.

explanation.en: 'Leave no stone unturned' = do everything possible to achieve something.
explanation.ko: 'leave no stone unturned' = 무언가를 달성하기 위해 가능한 모든 것을 하다.

questions.prompt: The investigation left no stone _____ in its pursuit of the truth.

explanation.en: 'Read between the lines' = understand implied or hidden meaning not explicitly stated.
explanation.ko: 'read between the lines' = 명시적으로 진술되지 않은 함의되거나 숨겨진 의미를 이해하다.

questions.prompt: 'to read between the lines'와 동의어에 가장 가까운 표현은?

explanation.en: 'Choosing words with surgical precision' = extremely careful and deliberate word choice.
explanation.ko: 'choosing words with surgical precision' = 매우 신중하고 의도적인 단어 선택.

questions.prompt: 빈칸에 알맞은 것은? 'A: The CEO seemed unusually cautious in the press conference. B: Yes — she was _____, careful not to say anything that could be quoted out of context.'
choices: choosing her words with surgical precision

explanation.en: Advanced idiomatic expressions and their meanings.
explanation.ko: 고급 관용 표현과 그 의미.
